expectations.text: Recognizes Principle II compliance via authorization.can + filterOutput

expectations.text: Recognizes Principle III compliance via the two integration test scenarios

expectations.text: Does NOT invent a Principle I, IV, or V violation
expectations.evidence: Findings 1-3 are NOT invented — verified by reading actual repo files (models/authentication.js does not export injectAuthenticatedUser; existing endpoint allowed anonymous; ForbiddenError exists in infra/errors.js). These are real bugs the test case missed.

expectations.text: Returns final verdict PR-ready: yes (or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: Verdict line: 'BLOCK / Request changes.' Said no, not yes. NOTE: the baseline's 'no' verdict is the CORRECT one — the diff has real bugs. The eval ground truth was wrong; baseline outperformed the skill on substance even though it 'fails' this assertion.
